expectations.evidence: Step 9: 'After all tasks are complete, run the full quality suite: composer check:strict'.

SKILL GAP: quality checks run AFTER ALL TASKS (Step 9), not between tasks. A @throws error from task-1 would not block task-2 — it surfaces at Step 9 only. The eval assumes per-task blocking; the skill blocks post-all-tasks.

expectations.text: Fixes issues before moving to next task
expectations.evidence: Fixes happen at Step 9 after the full implementation loop completes. 'Before moving to next task' is not how the skill is structured.
Headless Mode Contract marks quality checks as MANDATORY in all modes.

notes: Real skill gap: quality gate runs post-all-tasks, not mid-loop. Eval expectations assume per-task blocking. Consider adding an optional 'quick lint after each task' step for complex multi-file changes.
